topic.name: Tax and Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to taxation, education, and public service initiatives in the United States.

Directs the Secretary of Labor to establish the S Corporation Employee Ownership Assistance Office to foster increased employee ownership of S corporations.

Requires that such programs serve children whose family income is no higher than 200% of the poverty level.
6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to forgive the balance due on Direct Loans for public sector employees.
Revises income contingent Direct Loan repayment schedules for married borrowers.
7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the inclusion of unemployment compensation in gross income.
Allows tax-free distributions from retirement plans for unemployed individuals.
9: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to support the use of Native American languages as the primary language of instruction.
Requires grant applicants to present assurances that their schools can provide education primarily through a Native American language.

Document 7: This bill amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to terminate limitations on general Medicaid funding...
Document 8: Revises TRICARE (a Department of Defense [DOD] managed health care program) to authorize the Secretary of Defense to provide extended health care services...
Document 9: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act (SSA) to increase civil money penalties, criminal fines, and prison sentences for fraud and abuse under the SSA title XVIII (Medicare) program.
Document 10: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act (SSA) to increase criminal penalties for both felony and misdemeanor fraud under SSA titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid).

topic.name: Veterans' Disability and Care Benefits
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and programs aimed at improving disability compensation, healthcare, and support services for veterans.

Document 1: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation.
Compensation owed for mental health based on activities in theater.
Document 2: Reduces the level of difficulty required in the use of prostheses by veterans.
Authorizes additional veterans' disability compensation for service-disabled veterans.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to accept voluntary services performed by veterans at national cemeteries.
Involves veterans sharing their military service stories.
Document 4: Covers costs associated with the care of veterans at medical foster homes.
Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs in conducting the medical foster home program.
Document 5: Increases monthly subsistence allowance for veterans in vocational rehabilitation programs.
Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to pay subsistence allowances during employment services.
Document 6: Excludes from gross income of veterans with service-connected disabilities income imputed from student loan discharge.
Affects parents or cosigners of deceased veterans.
Document 7: Establishes centers for traumatic brain injury research and education.
Ensures geographic distribution of TBI care centers.
Document 8: Amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act regarding veteran hospital care costs.
Ensures veterans pay no more than they would at a VA facility for care.
Document 9: Contracts with non-VA facilities to provide services to veterans during major disasters.
Applies to VA facilities unable to provide services due to disasters.
Document 10: Directs the VA to pay for adult day health care for veterans not receiving nursing home care.
Payment must equal 65% of what the VA would pay for nursing home care.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Environmental Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating oil and gas exploration, production, and their environmental impacts.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
in carrying out any Act or program to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well
The EPA must issue a final rule adding hydrogen sulfide to the list of hazardous air pollutants.
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to revise the policy concerning resource development on the outer Continental Shelf
leasing, exploration, or development will be authorized in limited areas only when sound science shows that such activities can proceed with minimal risk.
Document 4: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency from devising or implementing a carbon tax

Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a program of awards to support the development, demonstration, and commercialization of innovative technologies to prevent, stop, or capture large-scale accidental discharges of oil
Requires the awards to focus on new technologies or innovative improvements to existing technologies.
Document 6: Authorizes the exploration, leasing, development, production, and transportation of oil and gas in and from the Coastal Plain in Alaska

Document 7: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations that require each Bureau of Ocean Energy Management employee to be subject to random testing
Prohibits the Secretary from exempting any category of activity of a vessel or facility engaged in OCS oil or gas drilling.
Document 8: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases

Document 9: Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well
Requires the Administrator to issue a final rule adding hydrogen sulfide to the list of hazardous air pollutants.
Document 10: Adds to the purpose of any grants by the Secretary of Labor to support a program to train workers in the construction, operation, maintenance, and performance of all related environmental processes
Increases from 15% to 50% the amount of federal funds that may be used for such training facility.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Reform

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish a nonimmigrant visa category (W-visa) for an alien coming to the United States to work as a professional nurse.
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to repeal the denial of an unlawful alien's eligibility for higher education benefits based on state residence.

Document 5: Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to declare that any unaccompanied alien child (UAC) who has not been a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons...
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish a nonimmigrant W-1 visa for an alien whose status in the United States is on a conditional basis.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to increase border patrol recruitment incentives...

Document 9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to provide increased protections and eligibility for family-sponsored immigrants.
Document 10: States that state and local law enforcement personnel... have the inherent authority to investigate, apprehend, or transfer to federal custody aliens in the United States.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focusing on the oversight and reporting of foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Land Conveyance and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the conveyance of federal lands for public use, economic development, and wilderness preservation.

Document 1: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness.
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take necessary measures in such wilderness to control fire, insects, and diseases.
Document 2: Authorizes the Department of the Army to convey all right, title, and interest of the United States in certain real property to the city of Copperas Cove, Texas.
Requires the city, in consideration for such conveyance, to convey all its right, title, and interest in real properties of equal fair market value that are acceptable to the Department.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to the city of Fruit Heights, Utah, all interest of the United States in approximately 100 acres of identified National Forest System land.
Requires the city to use such land only for public purposes.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to convey four specified parcels of land in Twin Falls, Idaho, to the city of Twin Falls for use to support public purposes.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey at current state of agricultural use market prices specified lands formerly part of the Fort Bayard Military Reservation, Grant County, New Mexico, to the Village of Santa Clara, the City of Bayard, and Grant County, New Mexico.
Provides that the Secretary shall be responsible for the costs and recovery of any historic or prehistoric cultural properties located on such lands.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, and the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM), to convey specified parcels of federal land to certain counties and cities in central Idaho.
Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas to be managed as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS).
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey, to the local communities in South Dakota that are currently managing specified community cemeteries, all interest of the United States in the parcels of National Forest System land that contain the cemeteries.
Ensures that such conveyances include unmarked gravesites and allow for the expansion of those cemeteries.
Document 8: Amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to adjust the boundaries of the North Cascades National Park and the Stephen Mather Wilderness in Washington state.
Requires that the boundary adjustments be such that equal acreage amounts are exchanged between the Wilderness and the Park.
Document 9: Transfers, upon the request of the city of Flagstaff, Arizona, to a specified parcel of land conveyed to the company any reversionary interest of the United States in and to a specified parcel of land.
Document 10: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS).
Authorizes land exchanges involving identified federal and non-federal lands in Humboldt County.

topic.name: Mental Health Services for Older Adults
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation aimed at improving mental health services and support systems for older adults, including integration into primary care and addressing substance abuse disorders.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings.

Requires the Director of the Center for Mental Health Services to designate a Deputy Director for Older Adult Mental Health Services to develop and implement initiatives to address the mental health needs of older adults.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities for projects to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings.
Require the inclusion of representatives of older adults and their families and geriatric mental health professionals on the Advisory Council for the Center.
Document 9: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings.
Directs the Secretary to give special consideration to providing treatment for older adults with substance abuse disorders when developing program priorities.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the regulation, safety, and monitoring of pharmaceuticals and controlled substances.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to allow the holder of an approved abbreviated new drug application to change the labeling of a drug.
Allows conforming changes to be ordered to the labeling of the equivalent listed drug.
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to include as a Schedule I controlled substance any material, compound, mixture, or preparation which contains specific psychoactive substances.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act and revises the Harold Rogers Prescription Drug Monitoring Program to require a state receiving grant funds for a prescription drug monitoring program to make relevant prescription information available.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to include postmarket safety activities within the process for the review of human drug applications.
Developing and using improved adverse event data collection systems and improved analytical tools to assess potential safety problems.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to make clerical corrections and eliminate certain restrictions on appropriated funds for reviewing medical device applications.
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require support for activities related to opioid abuse, including treatment services.
Requires the FDA to refer new drug applications for opioids without abuse deterrent properties to an advisory committee.
Document 7: Requires a state that receives a prescription drug monitoring program implementation and enhancement grant to facilitate interstate electronic information sharing about prescriptions for controlled substances.
Document 8: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to require an update of the Medicare physician fee schedule.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to declare that nothing shall prevent the importation, distribution, or sale of investigational drugs for terminally ill patients.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to permit brand name drug applications to rely upon investigations conducted in certain foreign countries.

topic.name: Water Resource Management and Ecosystem Restoration
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative acts aimed at managing water resources and restoring ecosystems in various regions of the United States.

Expand reclaimed water distribution, storage, and treatment facilities.
Document 4: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement.
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements.
Document 6: Authorize the Secretary of the Interior...to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.
Enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem.
Document 7: Prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control, water supply storage.
Identify demonstration projects for construction and restoration of the San Gabriel River Watershed.

topic.name: Housing Finance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating and improving the residential mortgage and housing finance system in the United States.

Document 1: Homeownership Protection and Housing Market Stabilization Act of 2008
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require additional creditor disclosures on residential mortgage loans.
Document 2: Directs each mortgage servicer to establish a deed-for-lease program
Requires a lease to carry a monthly rent amount equal to the fair market rent for the property.
Document 3: Requires each applicant for a single-family home mortgage to provide the lender information to verify the applicant's citizenship or alien status
newly insured under the Federal Housing Administration (FHA) mortgage insurance program.
Document 4: Encourages the states to establish a Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry
Sets forth procedures, requirements, and standards for mandatory registration and state licensing of mortgage loan originators.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to authorize the participation of mortgage brokers and lenders
as direct endorsement mortgagees under the FHA single family mortgage insurance program.
Document 6: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require each servicer of a home mortgage to respond in writing within 30 days
Authorizes an aggrieved individual to bring a civil action for damages and equitable relief.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury, the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae)
to enter into an agreement that modifies the Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement for the GSE.
Document 8: Authorizes a mortgagor of a property subject to a federally related mortgage loan to file a motion before a court

Document 9: Extends the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) demonstration projects on the guaranteeing of veterans' adjustable rate and hybrid adjustable rate mortgage loans

Document 10: Amends the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation Act and the Federal National Mortgage Association Charter Act
to increase limitations on the maximum original principal obligation of mortgages that may be purchased.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments and acts aimed at the conservation and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Document 2: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program.
The U.S. Coral Reef Task Force is authorized to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to the Secretary of Commerce.
Defines 'coral reef ecosystem' as the system of coral reefs and geographically associated species, habitats, and environment.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
NOAA and the Department of the Interior are authorized to undertake emergency actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or a coral reef ecosystem.
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds.
Authorizes the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to undertake or authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts.
Document 6: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 8: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 9: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Directs the Secretary to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 10: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Makes the destruction, loss, or injury of a coral reef not unlawful in certain circumstances.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: A collection of U.S. legislative acts aimed at promoting renewable energy technologies, reducing carbon emissions, and enhancing energy efficiency.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the protection and accountability of personally identifiable information and addressing data breaches.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to provide criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.

Requires a person who owns or possesses data in electronic form containing a means of identification to provide prompt notice to the U.S. Secret Service or Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) of a major security breach.
Document 2: Imposes a fine and/or prison term for intentionally concealing a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.
Makes it unlawful for a service provider to monitor, manipulate, aggregate, and market data from websites without the consent of the Internet user.
Document 4: Requires commercial entities to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data in electronic form containing personal information.
Directs a covered entity to give notice of any breach of security that the entity reasonably believes has caused or will cause identity theft.
Document 5: Requires commercial entities to notify the Secret Service or the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) of a security breach of personal information involving more than 10,000 individuals.
Sets forth the methods for notification under this Act.

6: impose a criminal penalty on any person who willfully misappropriates another person's taxpayer identity

8: impose a fine and/or prison term on any person who willfully misappropriates another person's taxpayer identity

10: prohibits hiring IRS employees with seriously delinquent tax debt

Document 9: authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to enter into agreements with persons for customs and immigration services at a land border port of entry
Document 10: requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for harbor maintenance programs to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund

topic.name: Regional Economic Development and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at fostering economic development, infrastructure improvement, and support for specific regions in the United States, particularly those facing economic distress or resource management challenges.

Document 1: Establishes the Northern Border Economic Development Commission
to develop plans and programs and to approve grants for the economic development of the region.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) and the Appalachian Regional Commission to carry out a program of outreach to veterans
to increase their awareness, access, and use of federal, state, and local programs.
Document 3: authorize the President agree to amendments to the Border Environment Cooperation Agreement
to expand the purposes and functions of the Border Environment Cooperation Commission.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a national cemetery in the Sarpy County, Nebraska, region
to serve the needs of veterans and their families.
Document 8: authorize specified additional projects, including projects for water conservation and improvement in Cameron, Hidalgo, Willacy, Hudspeth, and El Paso counties, Texas.
Permits each project that the Secretary of the Interior conducts or participates in to include: the replacement of irrigation canals.
Document 10: authorize the Bureau of Reclamation to participate in projects to benefit eligible communities through projects to capture and treat wastewater
and to distribute recycled water from a new mixed-use development.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming federal agencies and processes, including governance, health care, and regulatory frameworks.

1: Revises the federal charter of the Reserve Officers Association of the United States
Specifies that there shall be a minimum of three national executive committee members on the Association's national executive committee.
2: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to reduce from 14 years to 7 years the term of office of the members of the Board of Governors
Increases from two years to four years the post-service restriction which renders Board members ineligible to hold any office.
3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act limit the somatic cell count in all dairy products
Subjects any person that sells, transports, or offers for sale or transport any dairy product in violation of this Act to a civil penalty.
4: Makes an employee of a land management agency serving under a time-limited appointment in the competitive service eligible to compete for a permanent appointment
the employee's performance has been at an acceptable level.
5: Establishes a Government Reform Commission to examine the current configuration of executive agencies
review agency operational jurisdictions to determine whether areas of overlap exist.
6: Requires that an appointee to the position of Chief of the Army Medical Specialist Corps be of a rank not less than lieutenant colonel
7: Allows federal agencies to retain up to 25% of annual savings from the implementation of plans
Requires an agency to submit to the appropriate congressional committees a plan and an associated request to amend its approved operating budget.
8: Directs the surgeons general of the military departments to develop a joint soldier tracking system
Requires that the system be updated daily.
9: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to reduce from 14 years to 7 years the term of office of the members of the Board of Governors
Increases the membership of the Federal Open Market Committee from five to six members.
10: Directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to develop a joint disability evaluation system
establishing the disability rating, compensation, and benefits programs for the member so retired or separated.

topic.name: Legislation and Policy Reform
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and policy reforms aimed at addressing issues related to cybersecurity, education, veterans' affairs, and social welfare.

Document 1: Cybersecurity Public Awareness Act of 2013
summarizes major cyber incidents involving networks of executive agencies
Document 2: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act
requires Alexander Creek to file any amendments to its corporate charter
Document 3: amends the charter of the Gold Star Wives of America corporation
repeal the prohibition against any actions by the corporation
Document 4: authorizes payment under the Department of Veterans Affairs
travel expenses in connection with the medical examination, treatment, or care of a veteran
Document 5: Elimination of the Single Parent Tax Act of 2009
repeal the requirement that states impose a $25 annual fee for successful child support collection
Document 6: United States-China Market Engagement and Export Promotion Act
directs the Secretary of Commerce to provide grants to states
Document 7: Growing Education Achievement Training Academies for Teachers and Principals Act
authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to states
Document 8: Protect America's Cities from Government Blacklist Act of 2013
prohibits a federal agency from establishing or implementing a policy that discourages or prohibits the selection of a location for travel
Document 9: Clarence Gideon Full Access to Justice Act
establishes the Defender Office for Supreme Court Advocacy
Document 10: Fair and Cost Effective Federal Travel Act of 2009
prohibits any federal department or agency from establishing a travel or conference policy that takes into account the perception of a location

Determination of states that have chosen to opt-out

Enrollment for supplementary medical insurance under Medicare

topic.name: Wilderness and Land Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts related to the designation, protection, and management of wilderness areas and federal lands in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Washington State Wilderness Act of 1984
specifically allow the operation and maintenance of Green Mountain Lookout.
Document 2: Designates specified lands in Utah as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Sets forth administrative requirements for federal reservation of water rights.
Document 3: Designates as wilderness certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) lands

Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to offer for competitive sale by auction 8% of the federal lands
